segments.text: Today we're going to look at one of my favourite fish - the shark.
segments.translation: 今天，我们来看看我最喜欢的鱼——鲨鱼。

segments.text: As you know, sharks have a reputation for being very dangerous creatures capable of injuring or killing humans, and I'd like to talk about sharks in Australia.
segments.translation: 如你所知，鲨鱼以危险的生物著称，它可以伤害或杀死人类。我想谈谈澳大利亚的鲨鱼。

segments.text: Sharks are rather large fish, often growing to over ten metres and the longest sharks caught in Australia have reached sixteen metres.
segments.translation: 鲨鱼是庞大的鱼类，经常长到10米多长，在澳洲曾经捕获过最大的鲨鱼达16米。

segments.text: Sharks vary in weight with size and breed, of course, but the heaviest shark caught in Australia was a White Pointer - that weighed seven hundred and ninety-five kilograms - quite a size!
segments.translation: 鲨鱼的体重因尺寸和品种而不同。但是澳洲捕获的最重的鲨鱼是一只大白鲨，重达795公斤，真大啊！

segments.text: Sharks have a different structure to most fish: instead of a skeleton made of bone, they have a tough elastic skeleton of cartilage.
segments.translation: 鲨鱼与多数鱼类的骨架不同，它有结实、有弹性的软骨骨架，而不是骨头组成的骨架。

segments.text: Unlike bone, this firm, pliable material is rather like your nose, and allows the shark to bend easily as it swims.
segments.translation: 与骨头不同，这种结实、柔韧的材质很像你的鼻子。它使鲨鱼在游的时候轻松弯曲。

segments.text: The shark's skin isn't covered with scales, like other fish: instead the skin's covered with barbs, giving it a rough texture like sandpaper.
segments.translation: 鲨鱼的皮肤不像其他鱼类一样由鳞覆盖。相反，它的皮肤布满了倒钩，形成砂纸一样的粗糙质地。

segments.text: As you know, sharks are very quick swimmers.
segments.translation: 如你所知，鲨鱼是游泳健将。

segments.text: This is made possible by their fins, one set at the side and another set underneath the body, and the tail also helps the shark move forward quickly.
segments.translation: 这归功于它的鳍，一组在一侧，另一组在身体下面，尾巴也帮助鲨鱼快速的向前移动。

segments.text: Unlike other fish, sharks have to keep swimming if they want to stay at a particular depth, and they rarely swim at the surface.
segments.translation: 与其他鱼类不同，如果鲨鱼想停留在某一特定的深处就得不停的游，它们很少在海面游。

segments.text: Mostly, they swim at the bottom of the ocean, scavenging and picking up food that's lying on the ocean floor.
segments.translation: 多数情况下，它们在海底游弋，搜寻捕食海底的食物。

segments.text: While most other animals, including fish, hunt their prey by means of their eyesight, sharks hunt essentially by smell.
segments.translation: 多数动物，包括鱼类都用它们的视力捕食，而鲨鱼基本上靠嗅觉。

segments.text: They have a very acute sense of smell - and can sense the presence of food long before they can see it.
segments.translation: 它们的嗅觉十分敏锐，可以探测到视力范围很远以外的食物。

segments.text: In Australia, where people spend a lot of time at the beach, the government has realised that it must prevent sharks from swimming near its beaches.
segments.translation: 在澳洲，人们会经常去海滩，所以政府意识到应该防止鲨鱼游到海滩附近。

segments.text: As a result, they've introduced a beach-netting program.
segments.translation: 结果，他们引进了沙滩网计划。

segments.text: Beach-netting, or meshing, involves setting large nets parallel to the shore: this means that the nets on New South Wales beaches are set on one day.
segments.translation: 沙滩网或者建网是设置一张与海岸平行的大网。这意味着新南威尔士的海滩在一天建网，在第二天收网。

segments.text: And then lifted and taken out to sea on the next day.
segments.translation: 然后在第二天又把网拎起来，带到海上去。

segments.text: When shark-netting first began in 1939, only the Sydney metropolitan beaches were meshed - these beaches were chosen because beaches near the city are usually the most crowded with swimmers.
segments.translation: 捕鲨网初次于1939年建立，只在悉尼都市的海滩建网，因为这些海滩靠近城市，通常是游泳者最多的地方。

segments.text: Ten years later, in 1949, systematic meshing was extended to include the beaches to the south of Sydney.
segments.translation: 10年后，在1949年，建网系统扩展到了悉尼南部的海滩。

segments.text: As a result of the general success of the program in Sydney, shark-meshing was introduced to the state of Queensland around 1970.
segments.translation: 由于该工程在悉尼取得了普遍的成功，捕鲨网在1970年左右被引进到昆士兰州。

segments.text: The New Zealand authorities also looked at it, but considered meshing uneconomical - as did Tahiti in the Pacific.
segments.translation: 新西兰官方也看好它，但是认为建网不赚钱。太平洋的大溪地岛也是同样的看法。

segments.text: At around the same time,South Africaintroduced meshing to some of its most popular swimming beaches.
segments.translation: 同时，南非在一些受欢迎的海滩建网。

segments.text: When meshing began, approximately fifteen hundred sharks were caught in the first year.
segments.translation: 刚建网的时候，第一年捕获了大约1500只鲨鱼。

segments.text: However, this declined in the years that followed, and since that time, the average annual catch has been only about a hundred and fifty a year.
segments.translation: 然而接下来开始下滑，自从那时，每年平均捕获量只有150只。

segments.text: The majority of sharks are caught during the warmest months, from November to February, when sharks are most active and when both the air and the ocean are at their maximum temperature.
segments.translation: 大多数鲨鱼在11月到2月之间最暖和的月份被捕，那时是鲨鱼最活跃的季节，空气和海洋达到最高温度。

segments.text: Despite quite large catches, some people believe that shark meshing is not the best way to catch sharks.
segments.translation: 尽管捕捉量很大，但是一些人认为捕鲨网不是捕捉鲨鱼最好的方法。

segments.text: It's not that they think sharks are afraid of nets, or because they eat holes in them, because neither of these is true.
segments.translation: 他们认为鲨鱼不怕渔网或因为它们撕咬渔网，这些都是错误的。

segments.text: But meshing does appear to be less effective than some other methods, especially when there are big seas with high rolling waves and strong currents and anything that lets the sand move - the sand that's holding the nets down.
segments.translation: 但是建网不如其它方式有效，特别是惊涛骇浪和其他事情使沙子移动的时候，沙子会使网下沉。

segments.text: When this moves the nets will also become less effective.
segments.translation: 这种移动会使网失效。
